issuesFound.fix: Line 1020: .hero-image img { transition: none; } /* add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) */

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button in the hero section moves upward on hover via “transform: translateY(-1px)”, but no prefers‑reduced‑motion rule disables this motion, which can affect users sensitive to animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1068: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } /* add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) */

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: In `newsletterForm._onSubmit` the email address is validated with `validateField`, but when the validation fails the code never adds `aria‑invalid="true"` (or removes it on success). Assistive technologies therefore cannot convey the error to screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Discount‑code input validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: `discountCodeInput._onApply` validates the entered code but never marks the input with `aria‑invalid="true"` when the code is invalid, so screen‑reader users receive no error indication.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed (`_openInstance` / `_closeInstance`) the UI changes are announced via custom events, but no `aria‑live` region is updated, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the state change.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: After a search query returns suggestions, the listbox is populated (`suggestionListRenderer.render`) but no `aria‑live` region is updated, so users of assistive technology are not informed that new suggestions are available.

issuesFound.title: “Scroll‑to‑top” button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button that scrolls the page to the top is referenced but never given an `aria‑label` or visible text, making it impossible for screen‑reader users to discover its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation toggle button missing an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) receives `aria‑expanded` but never receives an accessible name (`aria‑label` or visible text). Users of assistive technology cannot identify its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger buttons lack an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger element for each dropdown receives `aria‑haspopup` and `aria‑expanded` but no accessible name, so screen‑reader users cannot know what the button controls.

issuesFound.title: Pricing‑period toggle buttons lack an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: Period selection buttons (`data-period`) receive `aria‑pressed` but no `aria‑label`, leaving their purpose ambiguous for screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle buttons lack an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons that switch between grid and list view receive `aria‑pressed` but no `aria‑label`, so their function is not announced.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle buttons lack an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that switch the site theme are given `aria‑pressed` but no `aria‑label`, making their purpose invisible to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register` the code only adds `aria-controls` when the panel already has an `id`. Most dropdown panels in the markup lack an `id`, so the trigger button ends up without an `aria-controls` reference. Screen‑reader users cannot discover which menu the button controls.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not marked `aria‑hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function hides the drawer by removing CSS classes but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element. When the drawer is hidden it remains in the accessibility tree, causing focus to land on hidden navigation links.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close actions not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` toggle the dropdown panel’s visibility but never write a status message to an `aria-live` region. Users of assistive technology receive no feedback that a menu has opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing `aria‑pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is a toggle that switches the navigation drawer on and off, but it only uses `aria-expanded`. For a button that represents a pressed/unpressed state, `aria‑pressed` is required to convey the toggle state to AT.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons lack proper tab‑list semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The group of period buttons (`_periodBtns`) is used as a mutually exclusive tab interface, but the container does not have `role="tablist"` and the buttons lack `role="tab"` and `aria-selected`. Without these roles, screen‑reader users cannot navigate the options as a tab set.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`_toggleEl`) controls the set of pricing cards, but no `aria-controls` attribute points to the region it manipulates. Assistive technologies cannot infer the relationship.

issuesFound.title: Removing filter chips does not announce the change
issuesFound.explanation: In `activeFilterChips._onChipClick` a chip is removed from the UI, but no live‑region announcement is made. Users of screen readers are unaware that a filter has been cleared.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard step changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard’s `goToStep` function updates the visible step but never writes a message to an `aria-live` region. Users navigating the form cannot know which step is now active.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not expose their selected state via `aria-selected`
issuesFound.explanation: Although the buttons receive `aria-pressed`, they are also used as a tab‑like selector. Without `aria-selected`, AT cannot convey which period is currently active.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack explicit `role="menu"` when used as navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is given `role="region"` only if the developer supplies it; many panels rely on default semantics. For keyboard‑driven navigation, `role="menu"` (or `listbox`) is required to expose the correct ARIA widget role.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav overlay missing `aria‑hidden` removal when shown

issuesFound.title: multi‑step wizard lacks a dedicated status element with `role="status"` for live announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard emits events but does not provide a persistent live‑region element for announcements, relying on `liveRegion.announce` which may be removed by other scripts.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger button lacks an accessible label describing its purpose when the visual label is icon‑only
issuesFound.explanation: If the trigger contains only an SVG icon, screen readers receive no descriptive text.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger button does not expose its expanded state via `aria‑pressed` (optional but recommended for toggle buttons)
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles a menu but only uses `aria‑expanded`. Adding `aria‑pressed` provides a second, widely‑supported state indicator.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The internal `_openInstance(inst)` function toggles the panel’s visibility but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `_openInstance` after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed the code emits custom events but does not write a message to any element with `role="status"`/`role="alert"` or `aria‑live`. Users of assistive technology receive no audible cue that the menu changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After showing the panel call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after hiding call `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` (or use a dedicated live‑region element).

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded true
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item, announceToSR)` function expands the answer panel but never updates `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button, so the expanded state is not exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls link to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`navToggle`) controls the off‑canvas drawer, but if the drawer element lacks an `id` the script does not add an `aria‑controls` reference, leaving the relationship implicit.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer has a unique `id` (e.g., `id="mobile-nav-drawer"`), and in `mobileNav.init` always call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);` regardless of whether the id existed beforehand.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle missing aria‑controls link to controlled region
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period switch (`data-billing-toggle`) controls the set of price panels but only receives `aria‑checked`. No `aria‑controls` attribute points to the region it updates, breaking the explicit relationship for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Give the price container an `id` (e.g., `id="price-panel"`), then in `pricing.init` add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', 'price-panel');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons missing aria‑controls link to carousel
issuesFound.explanation: The previous/next buttons (`[data-carousel-prev]`, `[data-carousel-next]`) move the carousel but lack `aria‑controls` that reference the carousel’s track element, so assistive technologies cannot associate the controls with the content they affect.
issuesFound.fix:
Assign an `id` to the carousel track (e.g., `id="testimonial-track"`), then set `setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-controls', 'testimonial-track');` and similarly for `_nextBtn`.

---

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard navigation buttons missing aria‑controls link to wizard panel
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard’s “Previous”, “Next”, and “Submit” buttons (`[data-wizard-prev]`, `[data-wizard-next]`, `[data-wizard-submit]`) control the wizard panels but have no `aria‑controls` attribute pointing to the container (`[data-wizard-steps]`). AT users cannot discover which region is being manipulated.
issuesFound.fix:
Give the steps container an `id` (e.g., `id="wizard-steps"`), then in `multiStepWizard.init` add `setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-controls', 'wizard-steps');` and the same for `_nextBtn` and `_submitBtn`.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter tab buttons missing aria‑controls link to filter panel
issuesFound.explanation: The same filter‑tab buttons also act as controls for the product grid, yet no `aria‑controls` attribute references the grid (`[data-product-grid]`). This omission prevents AT from understanding the relationship.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the product grid has an `id` (e.g., `id="product-grid"`), then in the tab‑click handler add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'product-grid');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel dot navigation buttons missing aria‑controls link to slides
issuesFound.explanation: The dot buttons (`[role="tab"]` inside `[data-carousel-dots]`) allow direct navigation to slides but lack `aria‑controls` that point to the corresponding slide element, breaking the required control‑panel relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Give each slide an `id` (e.g., `id="slide-1"`, `id="slide-2"` …) and set each dot’s `aria-controls` to the matching slide id when the dots are generated.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters updates the product‑count badge and empty‑state message without announcing the change via a live region.

After _updateCountBadge and _updateEmptyState, set statusEl.textContent (e.g., “12 products shown”) and setAttr(statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite'); or use liveRegion.announce().

issuesFound.title: productFilter view‑mode toggle button does not announce the new view mode via a live region.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel is hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed (`_closeInstance`), the script only adds a CSS class and later sets `hidden` attribute, but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. The panel stays in the accessibility tree until the `hidden` attribute takes effect, which may be delayed.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.fix:
In `inlineValidator._renderResult`, when `result.valid` is false, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when valid, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on contact‑form first‑name field
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form’s first‑name input (`#contact-first-name`) is validated via `inlineValidator.bind`, but the validator never adds `aria-invalid` on error, so screen readers cannot detect the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix:
Same as Issue 5 – ensure `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` on error and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge` changes the text of the element with `[data-product-count]` to reflect the number of matching products, but no live‑region announcement is made. Users of assistive technology are unaware of the new count.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the badge, set `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-live', 'polite')` (or use an existing live region) and optionally call `liveRegion.announce` with a message like “X products found”.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is changed (`pricing.setPeriod`), the UI updates the price display but does not provide an ARIA live announcement. Screen‑reader users cannot hear that the billing period changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the UI, call `liveRegion.announce` with a message such as “Billing period switched to annual”.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer lacks initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the `.nav-toggle` button does not have an explicit `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its collapsed state is not conveyed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.init` (or after element selection), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack initial aria‑hidden state
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are rendered without `aria-hidden="true"` when closed initially, so they are exposed to assistive technology even though they are invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In `dropdownMenu.init`, after setting `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')`, also call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; ensure `openInstance` removes the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes to open the drawer but never sets aria‑expanded on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside mobileNav.open() (and the corresponding false value in mobileNav.close()).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but never resets aria‑expanded to false on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside mobileNav.close().

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria‑expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() toggles the panel visibility but never updates the aria‑expanded state of the associated trigger element.
issuesFound.fix: After opening the panel, call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close does not clear aria‑expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance() hides the dropdown panel without resetting aria‑expanded to false on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded on question button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() adds the open class and animates the panel but never sets aria‑expanded on the trigger element, leaving assistive tech unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening a FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close does not clear aria‑expanded on question button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close() removes the open class and hides the panel but does not set aria‑expanded to false on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not set aria‑expanded when suggestions panel opens
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._open() marks the internal state as open and shows the suggestion list, but never updates aria‑expanded on the search <input>. Users of assistive technology cannot tell that a list is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening and `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The validator adds error messages and CSS classes but never sets aria‑invalid on the field, so screen readers do not receive the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate(), after a failed validation, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not remove aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field passes validation the code removes error styling but does not clear aria‑invalid, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: After a successful validation, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden with CSS transforms only, no aria‑hidden or hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is moved off‑canvas via CSS classes but remains in the accessibility tree because no aria‑hidden or hidden attribute is applied. Keyboard users can tab into hidden navigation items.
issuesFound.fix: When opening, call `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` and when closing, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle button does not expose its pressed state via aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles between grid and list views but never updates aria‑pressed, so screen‑reader users cannot know which view is active.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === 'grid' ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Assistive technologies assume the trigger is collapsed only if aria‑expanded is present and set to false.
issuesFound.fix: During initialization, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestions list does not announce opening via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the suggestion panel opens, no live‑region announcement is made, leaving users unaware that suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: After showing the list, call `liveRegion.announce('Search suggestions available');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the navigation drawer changes visible UI but no live‑region message is emitted, so screen‑reader users may miss the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened');` in open() and `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu closed');` in close().

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button used to open the mobile menu has no aria‑expanded attribute on page load, so its collapsed state is not conveyed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init() after locating _trigger, set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') (and optionally aria-controls pointing to the drawer’s id).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button does not have an aria‑controls attribute referencing the navigation drawer, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: After the drawer element is identified, ensure setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id) (where _drawer has a unique id).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick() toggles a dropdown panel’s visibility but never updates the trigger button’s aria‑expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and after closing, set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are created with aria‑haspopup but lack an initial aria‑expanded="false", so their collapsed state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.init() or when registering each trigger, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands an FAQ item but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving the expanded state hidden from screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ trigger buttons are given aria‑controls and role="button" but lack an initial aria‑expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: In faqRegistry.parse() or during faqAccordion.init(), set setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult() adds error messages but never sets aria‑invalid on invalid fields, violating ARIA error‑indication guidelines.
issuesFound.fix: When result.valid is false, call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); and when true, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates (product filter) not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters() updates the product grid and count badge but does not announce the result (e.g., “12 products shown”) to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: After filtering, call liveRegion.announce(`${visibleCount} products shown`) or similar.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates (pricing period change) not announced
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod() updates plan prices but provides no live‑region announcement, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, call liveRegion.announce(`Pricing switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`) .

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.open() and close() change the navigation drawer’s visibility but do not announce the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened'); and after closing, announce 'Navigation menu closed'.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() show/hide panels without live‑region announcements.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); and after closing, liveRegion.announce('Menu closed').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() and close() expand/collapse items without announcing the change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`); and after closing, liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS class removal without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes the open class from the drawer but never sets aria‑hidden="true", so the hidden drawer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In close(), add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in open(), remove that attribute.

Inside `_openInstance`, after `inst.open = true;` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Likewise, in `_closeInstance` set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button receives `aria-haspopup` but no `aria-controls` linking it to the panel, so the relationship is implicit.

After ensuring the panel has an ID (`panelId`), call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not manage aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion list is shown/hidden by setting internal state, but the `<input>` never receives `aria‑expanded="true"`/`"false"`, violating the combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_open()` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_close()` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels do not update aria‑expanded on their triggers
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened the script animates the panel but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button, so screen readers cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. In `close(item)` set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ triggers lack aria‑controls linking to panels
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger receives an ID and the panel receives an ID, but the code only sets `aria-controls` on the trigger if missing; however the condition `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls'))` may skip when the attribute already exists with a wrong value. Ensure the correct ID is always set.
issuesFound.fix:
Replace the conditional with `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);` unconditionally.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel period buttons lack aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (`_periodBtns`) are used as a tab‑list but never receive `aria‑pressed` or `aria‑selected`, so their state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_syncToggleState(period)` add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period));` for each button, and set the initial state on load.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑pressed and aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period buttons (`_periodBtns`) act as a toggle group but are not given `aria‑pressed` (or `aria‑selected`) to indicate the active period.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating `_periodBtns`, call `_syncToggleState(_pricingState.period);` which should also set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', isActive ? 'true' : 'false');`. Ensure the function updates the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑toggle switch does not expose aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off switch (`_toggleEl`) toggles the billing period but never updates `aria‑pressed`, leaving its state ambiguous to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard navigation buttons lack aria‑controls and aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The “Previous” and “Next” buttons move focus between steps but do not indicate which step panel they control via `aria-controls`, nor do they expose their enabled/disabled state with `aria-disabled`.
issuesFound.fix:
When initializing, set `setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_steps[0].panelEl, 'step-0'));` and similarly for each step. Also set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-disabled', btn.disabled ? 'true' : 'false');` whenever the button state changes.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone does not associate the drop zone with the file list via aria‑describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The drag‑and‑drop zone (`zoneEl`) creates a file list (`listEl`) but never links the zone to the list with `aria-describedby`, so screen‑reader users cannot discover the list of selected files.
issuesFound.fix: After creating `listEl`, add `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-describedby', srUtils.ensureId(listEl, 'upload-list'));`. Also ensure the list has `role="list"` and each entry `role="listitem"` (already present).
